hero.tagline: I solve human problems with tech through an ethical approach.

about.heading: Hi, I'm Mar.
about.bio_p2: Political Science graduate, passionate about technology and the human problems hiding inside everyday operations. I have two girl dogs who are the best part of my life.
about.bio_p3: I'm an operator who thinks strategically. For 5+ years I've owned product and operational initiatives end-to-end — from the first conversation to the system running in production. My job is reducing ambiguity, creating alignment, and turning ideas into something teams can actually run on.
about.bio_p4: I care about people, and I show that through structure and clarity, not slogans.

items.description: Take leadership objectives and turn them into roadmaps teams can actually run. Own incident response end-to-end — when a client-facing backend incident hit, I ran discovery, defined the fix, and presented the methodology change directly to the client as their single point of contact. Built and lead Agent Hub (10+ AI agents), authored the Imagine Apps Ecosystem internal reference, and built the Learning Hub for company onboarding.
Manage academic programs and guide large student groups through structured learning processes and project-based activities.

Owned the product roadmap for a distributed international team. Led the daily negotiation between what the business needed and what engineering could realistically ship. Ran requirements conversations directly with stakeholders — no relay, no telephone game.
This organization didn't have a project management framework — I built one, along with the KPI tracking to know if it was actually working.

Managed distributed teams delivering web and mobile products across time zones. Owned sprint planning, backlog, risk and dependency tracking. When something was about to break, I wanted to know before the client did.
Supported operational scaling and internal process improvements for a remote-first organization.

projects.heading: What I've
projects.subtitle: Get to know the projects I've built so far. You'll be able to see what I've done inside and outside my job. Also, what I've tried — check out my Attempts Gallery.

agent-hub.title: Agent Hub — Friction Reduction AI Agent Interface
agent-hub.problem: The bottleneck wasn't building AI agents — it was that non-technical users couldn't access them without friction. The technology existed; the human interface didn't.
agent-hub.built: Defined the product vision, built and curated 10+ agents, and designed an interface that eliminates access friction for non-technical users. Led the full backlog and took the project to external pitch.
agent-hub.impact: From internal tool to a commercially viable proposal. Agent Hub shifted the conversation from 'we have agents' to 'anyone can use them'.

imagine-apps-ecosystem.title: Imagine Apps Ecosystem — Internal Knowledge System
imagine-apps-ecosystem.problem: Organizations break when knowledge lives in people's heads instead of systems. Every departure is a loss of institutional memory.
imagine-apps-ecosystem.built: Comprehensive system centralizing all of Imagine Apps' operational knowledge: Agents Hub, Ops Hub, and Learning Hub. A single source of truth built to outlast any individual.
imagine-apps-ecosystem.impact: The company stopped depending on specific people to function. Knowledge became transferable, scalable, and auditable.

finanz-butik-helpdesk.title: Finanz Butik Helpdesk
finanz-butik-helpdesk.problem: The team managed scattered requests without traceability, causing lost tickets, unclear priorities, and fragmented communication.
finanz-butik-helpdesk.built: Centralized hub with guided 6-step form, automatic ticket classification with confidence scoring and unique nomenclature. Direct Basecamp integration. Full Kanban with RBAC.
finanz-butik-helpdesk.impact: System deployed in production with beta users processing real tickets. Eliminated request scattering and created complete lifecycle traceability.

gimena-user-story-writer.title: Gimena — Agentic User Story Writer
gimena-user-story-writer.problem: Converting Figma designs and business requirements into development-ready User Stories was a manual, slow, and error-prone process.
gimena-user-story-writer.built: AI agent that transforms Figma flows and business requirements into development-ready User Stories. Automatically identifies edge cases to prevent rework.
gimena-user-story-writer.impact: Accelerated Imagine Apps' delivery cycle. PMs can focus on strategy while Gimena handles technical breakdown.

lia-productivity-hub.title: Lía — The Productivity & Visibility Hub
lia-productivity-hub.problem: Teams were managing calendars, tasks, and sessions in separate tools, losing visibility and context between them.
lia-productivity-hub.built: Unified planner integrating calendars, tasks, and sessions into a single visibility interface. Validated as a daily-use solution at ImagineApps.
lia-productivity-hub.impact: Validated as a successful POC. Evolved into a scalable asset with potential to become a modular product for external teams.

operations-evolution-dashboard.title: Operations Evolution Dashboard
operations-evolution-dashboard.problem: Project management relied on verbal updates. Without metrics, prioritization decisions were subjective and stakeholder visibility was nearly nonexistent.
operations-evolution-dashboard.built: Operational dashboard transforming verbal reports into measurable metrics: completion probability, requests by area, minimum reporting requirements per project and person.
operations-evolution-dashboard.impact: New single source of truth for global statuses. Enabled transparent stakeholder priority alignment based on historical data.

ai-wiki-for-builders.title: AI Wiki for Builders
ai-wiki-for-builders.problem: People starting to use AI to build products don't need another tutorial — they need ready tools to solve real problems, now.
ai-wiki-for-builders.built: Curated collection of skills, agents, and agentic structures designed to solve problems in real time. Every resource was tested before inclusion.
ai-wiki-for-builders.impact: Independent resource for builders who prefer learning by doing. Reduces the entry curve from weeks to hours.

mujeres-builders.title: Mujeres Builders — Women-in-Tech Community
mujeres-builders.problem: The community had content volume but no identity. The real problem wasn't the number of posts — women weren't co-creating, just consuming.
mujeres-builders.built: Database of 28 AI tools organized for the community. Strategic content and engagement framework centered on shared identity and co-creation, not visibility metrics.
mujeres-builders.impact: Active network of 100+ women collaborating and building. The community went from audience to team.

speaker-ai-summit.title: Speaker — AI Summit
speaker-ai-summit.problem: Companies wanted to implement AI but confused the tool with the strategy. The problem wasn't access to technology — it was knowing what to build with it.
speaker-ai-summit.built: AI implementation Master Class for organizations across different industries, together with Imagine Apps and 30X. Designed for executives and leaders, not technical teams.
speaker-ai-summit.impact: Business audience with concrete use cases and a framework to evaluate where AI implementation makes sense in their operations.

finanz-butik-release-notes.title: Finanz Butik — Release Notes & Product Communication
finanz-butik-release-notes.problem: The client came to Scrum ceremonies with questions, not judgment. Technical changes were opaque for a non-technical audience, turning sprint reviews into interrogations.
finanz-butik-release-notes.built: Release notes decks translating technical changes into business language — without oversimplifying or omitting. Each delivery is designed for the client to arrive at the ceremony with a critical stance.
finanz-butik-release-notes.impact: The client went from asking questions to making observations. Ceremonies became strategic conversations, not explanation sessions.

growth-plan.title: Growth Plan — Structured Professional Development
growth-plan.problem: Professional growth without structure becomes reactivity — you learn what's urgent, not what's strategic. Without a roadmap, you go in circles.
growth-plan.built: Personal and professional development plan with upskilling priorities and career trajectory, built with professional mentorship. Includes skills map, cycle objectives, and progress metrics.
growth-plan.impact: Trajectory from PM to Strategic Lead based on deliberate decisions, not accidental opportunities. An own growth system that remains active.

items.relevance: Advanced project management methodologies and professional certification.

items.relevance: Foundation in political theory, public policy, governance, and social systems.

contact.body: I'm open to new projects, collabs, and conversations. Write to me — I respond to everything.
